Strategy 1: Refactor the loop-condition write locally. Change `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation, evaluation_extra)` to `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation)`, and insert a new activity at the end of the loop body, after the existing evaluation exclusive block: `Determine whether extra due diligence is required (write: evaluation_extra)`. Keep the loop as `loop (cond: data.evaluation_extra) (mode=pre_test, cond=data.evaluation_extra)`. This prevents `conduct due diligence study` from setting `data.evaluation_extra` after the relevant loop-condition check, while still allowing the new decision activity to trigger another loop iteration in which `conduct due diligence study` eventually executes. This preserves R1 and R3.

Strategy 2: If `conduct due diligence study` does not need to control repetition of the due-diligence loop, simply remove `evaluation_extra` from its write set: replace `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation, evaluation_extra)` with `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation)`. Then `data.evaluation_extra` is no longer caused by an activity that is not prior to the loop branch. Whenever the loop condition `data.evaluation_extra` is true, the loop body still executes `conduct due diligence study`. This is the smallest change, but it removes the ability of the study itself to request another due-diligence iteration.

Strategy 3: Replace the pre-test loop with a guarded post-test loop. Wrap the loop in an exclusive branch: one branch with condition `data.evaluation_extra` contains a `loop (mode=post_test, cond=data.evaluation_extra)` whose body is the current loop body, and the other branch with condition `not data.evaluation_extra` is empty. With a post-test loop, any update of `data.evaluation_extra` by `conduct due diligence study` is evaluated after the activity, and if it remains true the next iteration will execute `conduct due diligence study` again. The external guard preserves the current behavior that no due-diligence loop is entered when `data.evaluation_extra` is initially false. R1 and R3 remain unchanged.

Strategy 4: Add an explicit follow-up execution after `conduct due diligence study` when it sets `data.evaluation_extra`. Immediately after `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation, evaluation_extra)`, insert an exclusive block with one branch `cond: data.evaluation_extra` containing `conduct due diligence study (write: evaluation)` and one empty branch `cond: not data.evaluation_extra`. The second occurrence should not write `evaluation_extra`. This ensures that if the first study makes `data.evaluation_extra` true, another `conduct due diligence study` eventually follows. This is less clean because it may duplicate due-diligence work, but it is a localized mitigation and does not affect R1 or R3.
